text: Why did the writer become very worried?
zh: 为什么这位作者变得非常担心?

text: Dentists always ask questions when it is impossible for you to answer.
zh: 牙医总是在你无法回答的时候提问。

text: My dentist had just pulled out one of my teeth and had told me to rest for a while.
zh: 我的牙医刚给我拔了一颗牙,让我休息一会儿。

text: but my mouth was full of cotton wool.
He knew I collected match boxes and asked me whether my collection was growing.
zh: 他知道我收集火柴盒,就问我的收藏是不是又增加了。

text: He then asked me how my brother was and whether I liked my new job in London.
zh: 接着他又问我哥哥怎么样,还问我喜不喜欢在伦敦的新工作。

text: In answer to these questions I either nodded or made strange noises.
zh: 面对这些问题,我要么点点头,要么发出奇怪的声音。

text: Meanwhile, my tongue was busy searching out the hole where the tooth had been.
zh: 与此同时,我的舌头一直在忙着探查那颗牙原来所在的洞。

text: I suddenly felt very worried, but could not say anything.
zh: 我突然感到非常担心,却什么也说不出来。

text: When the dentist at last removed the cotton wool from my mouth,
zh: 当牙医终于把棉花从我嘴里取出来时,

text: I was able to tell him that he had pulled out the wrong tooth.
zh: 我才得以告诉他,他拔错了牙。
